title: Aurelia Jeffery Plays With Toy Trucks Inside the Iconic Llao Llao Hotel Bariloche Rio Negro
description: Llao Llao, Río Negro: Aurelia Jeffery explores the interior of the storied hotel with a collection of plastic toy trucks. The natural light filtering through the large window illuminates the rustic wooden floorboards, casting soft shadows that highlight the textures of the room. A vintage-inspired radiator stands against the wall, reflecting the traditional architecture found throughout this historic mountain retreat. The composition focuses on a quiet, candid moment of childhood curiosity amidst the luxurious, timeless backdrop of the Andean landscape. Her engagement with her toys serves as a grounding human element within the grand scale of this Patagonian landmark. Every detail, from the vibrant hula hoops resting against the wall to the chalk-filled blackboard, suggests a space where legacy and family comfort intersect seamlessly. The shallow depth of field isolates the subject, allowing the blurred historical interior to frame her innocent exploration. This scene captures the intersection of world-class hospitality and the intimate, everyday rhythms of family travel in the Argentine south. Travelers often seek out such destinations not just for their scenic beauty, but for their ability to provide a warm, hospitable sanctuary during extended journeys through remote wilderness regions. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.
